what_it_does: The HTML entry page for the Virtual Hub UI kit viewer. It sets up a single #app mount, loads React 18.3.1, ReactDOM, and Babel-standalone from unpkg CDNs, then loads eight type='text/babel' .jsx kit files (HubBug, MenuButton, QuickMenu, InfoPanel, SharePanel, FloorplanOverlay, CaptureComment, HubApp) to be transpiled in-browser. Links two stylesheets (colors_and_type.css and hub.css).
observations: Carries a @dsCard HTML comment annotation describing the gallery card: group 'UI Kit — Virtual Hub', name, subtitle 'Buyer-side panotour overlay · MENU · Info · Floorplan · Capture', viewport '1280x720'
Loads React, ReactDOM, and Babel from unpkg.com with SRI integrity hashes and crossorigin='anonymous'
Eight component scripts loaded as type='text/babel' (in-browser transpilation), HubApp.jsx loaded last as the orchestrator/mount

Empty
as the mount point
Links ../../colors_and_type.css (the design-token stylesheet) and local hub.css
Component load order: building blocks first, HubApp.jsx last
standouts: @dsCard metadata comment driving an external gallery rendering
In-browser Babel transpilation of all .jsx — a no-build prototype harness
SRI integrity + crossorigin on every CDN script
conventions: Follows the claude-ds kit pattern of a Babel-in-browser viewer linking the shared colors_and_type.css token stylesheet
Component scripts ordered building-blocks-first, app-orchestrator-last (matches the charter's load-order discipline)
Pins exact CDN versions (react@18.3.1, babel@7.29.0) with integrity hashes
Uses external CDN dependencies, which differs from a fully self-contained artifact
concerns: Ships React development builds and runs Babel transpilation at runtime — viewer/prototype-grade, not production-optimized
Hard dependency on unpkg.com availability for the kit to render
notes: The no-build viewer page that assembles the Virtual Hub buyer-side overlay kit by Babel-transpiling eight .jsx components into a single #app mount.
questions: Is hub.css local to this kit and colors_and_type.css the shared token source?
Does HubApp.jsx perform the createRoot mount into #app?
Is the @dsCard comment consumed by the design folder's gallery.py or a separate claude-ds gallery?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the @dsCard subtitle and title accurately describe a buyer-side panotour overlay UI kit viewer with MENU/Info/Floorplan/Capture.
apparent_themes: Component-assembly viewer for a UI kit
Design-token reuse via shared stylesheet linking
intention_signals: Aims to provide a zero-build standalone viewer that stitches the virtual-hub overlay components together for visual review, self-describing as a gallery card and reusing the shared token stylesheet.
novelty: The @dsCard annotation comment that lets the page self-describe as a gallery entry (group/name/subtitle/viewport).
contribution: Contributes the buyer-side panotour overlay assembly — the manifest of which eight components compose the Virtual Hub viewer and the gallery-card metadata that catalogs it; a fused system would keep this as the kit's entry/composition point and its self-describing card.
summary_for_embedding: An HTML viewer page that Babel-transpiles eight React .jsx components in-browser to render the buyer-side Virtual Hub panotour overlay UI kit, with a @dsCard gallery annotation and shared token stylesheet links.
intention_for_embedding: To assemble and visually preview the Virtual Hub buyer-side overlay UI kit as a no-build, self-describing gallery card.
